MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2022 Off Calendar

MOTION TO BIFURCATE/SEVER The Issue Of Duty Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 598 And 1048(B)
Off Calendar Mar-14-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Bill Daniels, Esq. (818.907.8073; Bill@DanielsLaw.com) for Plaintiff.
Angela Marwith, Esq. (714.907.6389; amarkwith@wfbm.com) of WFBM, LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION TO BIFURCATE/SEVER The Issue Of Duty Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 598 And 1048(B).

Defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Company's motion to sever or bifurcate the issue of duty is denied without prejudice to re-raising the motion with the trial judge. The trial judge is in the best position to determine whether and, if so, how the trial should be divided into phases. Moreover, the relief sought by PG&E is not clear and arguably did not provide adequate notice to plaintiff Robert Lambing to enable him to meaningfully oppose the motion. In its notice of motion and initial memorandum PG&E repeatedly refers to the issue it wants to be decided first as whether it owed any duty to Dr. Lambing and contended that a resolution in its favor would end the case. However, in its reply memorandum PG&E says the issue it wants decided first is whether it owed a duty greater than that imposed on it by CPUC's orders, which would not end the case, regardless of how that issue was decided. Without acknowledging that it shifted focus on this motion, PG&E's reply memorandum chides Dr. Lambing for having confused the issue on this motion.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
